creator: maciej@suszko.eu
text:
In my case, every time the message appears in the log, snmpd daemon
eats a few bytes of memory, so it definately looks like there is some
memory leak.

After about 3 hours of runnning simple snmpwalk test, the
ps command shows the memory usage difference:

walk.sh script:
#!/bin/sh

while true; do
    ps axo vsz,rss,comm | grep snmpd
    snmpwalk -v1 -cpublic localhost .1 >/dev/null 2>&1
done


tlhscd@machine:~ $ ./walk.sh
32980   5548 snmpd
32980   6456 snmpd
32980   6492 snmpd
...
155860  11356 snmpd
155860  11356 snmpd


snmpd.conf:
agentaddress 127.0.0.1
smuxsocket 127.0.0.1
com2sec local 127.0.0.0/8 public
group MyROGroup v1 local
group MyROGroup v2c local
group MyROGroup usm local
view all included .1 80
access MyROGroup "" any noauth exact all none none
syslocation somewhere
syscontact admin <admin@machine>


/var/db/ports/net-snmp/options:
# This file is auto-generated by 'make config'.
# No user-servicable parts inside!
# Options for net-snmp-5.7.1_7
_OPTIONS_READ=net-snmp-5.7.1_7
WITHOUT_IPV6=true
WITHOUT_MFD_REWRITES=true
WITHOUT_PERL=true
WITHOUT_PERL_EMBEDDED=true
WITHOUT_PYTHON=true
WITHOUT_DUMMY=true
WITHOUT_TKMIB=true
WITHOUT_DMALLOC=true
WITHOUT_MYSQL=true
WITHOUT_AX_SOCKONLY=true
WITHOUT_UNPRIVILEGED=true
-- 
regards, Maciej Suszko.
